document.title: Usability Validation Plan for G Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Usability validation plan for G Series digital flat panel X-ray detector models.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Defines planned usability test participants, scenarios, metrics, goals, acceptance criteria, and reporting for the listed G Series detector models.
keyPoints: The document describes a test plan for conducting a usability test during development of G1417CW, G1717CW, G1417FW, G1717FW, G1417CWP, G1717CWP, G1417FWP, and G1717FWP.
The plan states performance and usage are the same except for size, with no difference in usability.
Seven participants are specified for the usability test.
Scenario completion success rates, adherence to dialog scripts, error rates, subjective evaluations, and time-to-completion will be used as usability metrics.
The usability goal is a total completion rate of 100% for each usability task and total errors below 4 for each usability task.
The references include EN 60601-1-6:2010, EN 62366-1:2015, IEC TR 62366-2:2016, and EN ISO 14971:2019.

concepts.name: Usability validation plan
concepts.evidence: This document describes a test plan for conducting a usability test during the development of the G1417CW/G1717CW/G1417FW/G1717FW/G1417CWP/G1717CWP/G1417FWP/G1717FWP.

concepts.name: Usability test objectives
concepts.evidence: The usability test objectives are: To construct the benchmarks of user attributes; To identify potential design problems to be solve; To improve product design and production efficiency; To complete end-user satisfaction maximization

concepts.name: Participants
concepts.evidence: A total 7 participants should be at least equal to the following User Profile used in the Usability test.

concepts.name: Usability metrics
concepts.evidence: Usability metrics refers to user performance measured against specific performance goals necessary to satisfy usability requirements.

concepts.name: Scenario completion
concepts.evidence: The scenario is completed when the participant indicates the scenario's goal has been obtained

concepts.name: Critical errors
concepts.evidence: Critical errors are unresolved errors during the process of completing the task or errors that produce an incorrect outcome regarding the safety.

concepts.name: Non-critical errors
concepts.evidence: Non-critical errors are an error that would not have an impact on the final output of the task but would result in the task being completed less efficiently.

concepts.name: Subjective evaluations
concepts.evidence: Subjective opinions about specific tasks, time to perform each task, features, and functionality will be surveyed and collected via questionnaires.

concepts.name: Hazard-related use scenarios
concepts.evidence: hazard-related use scenarios for summative evaluation

concepts.name: Completion rate
concepts.evidence: Completion rate is the percentage of test participants who successfully complete the task without critical errors.

concepts.name: Usability verification and evaluation report
concepts.evidence: The Usability verification & evaluation report will be provided at the conclusion of the usability test.

evidence.claim: The document is a usability validation plan for the G Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Usability Validation Plan for G Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The report number and revision are A-TCF-10-106 (Rev.0).
The document was prepared, reviewed, and approved on Nov 25, 2024.
evidence.evidenceText: Prepared R&D team Park, Jaehong Nov 25, 2024; Review R&D Manager Lee, Dongho Nov 25, 2024; Approval Quality Manager Hur Namkyu Nov 25, 2024
Rev. No. 0 Rev. date Nov 25, 2024 Revision contents Establishment

evidence.claim: The usability test plan applies to G1417CW, G1717CW, G1417FW, G1717FW, G1417CWP, G1717CWP, G1417FWP, and G1717FWP.
evidence.evidenceText: This document describes a test plan for conducting a usability test during the development of the G1417CW/G1717CW/G1417FW/G1717FW/G1417CWP/G1717CWP/G1417FWP/G1717FWP.
evidence.pageTarget: G Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The listed detector accessories have the same performance and usage except for size, so there is no usability difference.
evidence.evidenceText: Performance and usage are all the same except for size, so there is no difference in 'Usability'

evidence.claim: The usability test uses seven participants.
evidence.evidenceText: A total 7 participants should be at least equal to the following User Profile used in the Usability test.

evidence.claim: Participants should have at least two years of university education, legal qualification for each country, English understanding, and no required special experience.
evidence.evidenceText: Education: At least two years of university, equal or more; Knowledge: User who meet the legal qualification for each country; Language understanding: User who are understand the English; Experience: Not require any special experience

evidence.claim: Participants will sign informed consent acknowledging voluntary participation and the right to stop at any time.
evidence.evidenceText: Participants will sign an informed consent that acknowledges: the participation is voluntary, that participation can cease at any time.

evidence.claim: Usability metrics include scenario completion success rates, adherence to dialog scripts, error rates, subjective evaluations, and time-to-completion.
evidence.evidenceText: Scenario completion success rates, adherence to dialog scripts, error rates, and subjective evaluations will be used. Time-to-completion of scenarios will also be collected.

evidence.claim: Critical errors include unresolved task errors or errors producing an incorrect outcome regarding safety.
evidence.evidenceText: Critical errors are unresolved errors during the process of completing the task or errors that produce an incorrect outcome regarding the safety.

evidence.claim: Non-critical errors do not affect final task output but make task completion less efficient.
evidence.evidenceText: Non-critical errors are an error that would not have an impact on the final output of the task but would result in the task being completed less efficiently.

evidence.claim: The post-test satisfaction questionnaire uses a 5-point scale from 1 strongly disagree to 5 strongly agree.
evidence.evidenceText: Rating: The 5-point rating scale ranged from 1 (Strongly disagree) to 5 (Strongly agree)

evidence.claim: Primary operating scenarios include user manual familiarization, power cord and adapter connection, LAN and PC connection, detector power-on, patient positioning, and detector calibration by PC software.
evidence.evidenceText: Familiarize yourself with the user manual before and after the product; Connecting the Power cord to the AC/DC adapter; LAN Cable and PC main body connection; Power on the Detector; Arrange the patient in the correct position to have Detector aligned with the target body part; Calibration of Detector by S/W in PC

evidence.claim: Hazard-related use scenarios include user manual checking, preparation, product use, X-ray image implementation, maintenance, cleaning, and battery charging and change.
evidence.evidenceText: hazard-related use scenarios for summative evaluation; Check the User manual; Preparation for using the product; Product use; Implementation of X-ray image; Maintenance; Cleaning; Battery charging and change

evidence.claim: The usability goal for each task is a total completion rate of 100%.
evidence.evidenceText: Usability Goal: A total completion rate of 100 % is the goal for each usability task.

evidence.claim: The usability goal for each task is total errors below 4.
evidence.evidenceText: Usability Goal: Total errors of below 4 is the goal for each usability task.

evidence.claim: A usability verification and evaluation report will be provided at the conclusion of the usability test.
evidence.evidenceText: The Usability verification & evaluation report will be provided at the conclusion of the usability test.

evidence.claim: The document references EN 60601-1-6:2010, EN 62366-1:2015, IEC TR 62366-2:2016, and EN ISO 14971:2019.

reviewFlags.reason: No regulatory authority or jurisdiction is identified in the source text; authoritySignal is general.
Several acceptance criteria state 'over 100% of operators', which is ambiguous and may require source document review.
The source metadata productSignal is Unclassified, although the source text lists G Series detector models.
